questions.question: What are the Ante-Nicene Fathers known for?
questions.answer: The Ante-Nicene Fathers were early Christian writers and theologians who lived before the First Council of Nicaea in 325 AD.

questions.question: What is the significance of the Council of Nicaea in Christian history?
questions.answer: The Council of Nicaea was the first ecumenical council of the Christian Church, held in 325 AD. It was significant for establishing the Nicene Creed.

questions.question: Who was the first Christian martyr after the death of Christ?
questions.answer: Saint Stephen is traditionally considered the first Christian martyr.

questions.question: What is Yom Kippur?
questions.answer: Yom Kippur is the Jewish Day of Atonement, a solemn day dedicated to repentance and seeking forgiveness.

questions.question: What is the biblical reference for 'In the beginning God created the heaven and the earth'?
questions.answer: Genesis 1:1

questions.question: What is the biblical reference for 'For God so loved the world, that he gave his only begotten Son'?
questions.answer: John 3:16

questions.question: What is the biblical reference for 'The Lord is my shepherd; I shall not want'?
questions.answer: Psalm 23:1

questions.question: What is the biblical reference for 'I am the way, the truth, and the life'?
questions.answer: John 14:6

questions.question: What is the biblical reference for 'Judge not, that ye be not judged'?
questions.answer: Matthew 7:1

questions.question: What is the biblical reference for 'Ask, and it shall be given you; seek, and ye shall find'?
questions.answer: Matthew 7:7

questions.question: What is the biblical reference for 'Greater love hath no man than this, that a man lay down his life for his friends'?
questions.answer: John 15:13

questions.question: What is the biblical reference for 'I can do all things through Christ which strengtheneth me'?
questions.answer: Philippians 4:13

questions.question: What is the biblical reference for 'Love thy neighbour as thyself'?
questions.answer: Leviticus 19:18 (also Matthew 22:39)

questions.question: What is the biblical reference for 'And now abideth faith, hope, charity, these three; but the greatest of these is charity'?
questions.answer: 1 Corinthians 13:13

questions.question: What are the first five books of the Hebrew Bible called?
questions.answer: The Torah (or Pentateuch)

questions.question: What is Hanukkah?
questions.answer: The Jewish Festival of Lights, commemorating the rededication of the Second Temple

questions.question: What Jewish holiday commemorates the Exodus from Egypt?
questions.answer: Passover (or Pesach)

questions.question: On what day of the week does Shabbat begin?
questions.answer: Friday evening (at sunset)

questions.question: At what age does a Jewish boy celebrate his Bar Mitzvah?
questions.answer: 13 years old

questions.question: What term describes food that is prepared according to Jewish dietary laws?
questions.answer: Kosher

questions.question: What is the Talmud?
questions.answer: The central text of Rabbinic Judaism, containing rabbinical discussions and interpretations of the Torah

questions.question: What are the Jewish High Holy Days?
questions.answer: Rosh Hashanah (New Year) and Yom Kippur (Day of Atonement)

questions.question: What is a menorah?
questions.answer: A seven-branched candelabrum that is a symbol of Judaism

questions.question: What is a rabbi?
questions.answer: A Jewish religious leader and teacher

questions.question: When was the First Council of Nicaea held?
questions.answer: 325 AD

questions.question: What was the primary issue addressed at the Council of Ephesus in 431 AD?
questions.answer: The nature of Christ and declaring Mary as Theotokos (Mother of God)

questions.question: What did the Council of Chalcedon in 451 AD declare about Christ?
questions.answer: That Christ has two natures - fully divine and fully human - in one person

questions.question: What was addressed at the Second Council of Nicaea in 787 AD?
questions.answer: The veneration of icons (rejecting iconoclasm)

questions.question: What major event was the Council of Trent (1545-1563) a response to?
questions.answer: The Protestant Reformation (it was part of the Counter-Reformation)

questions.question: What doctrine was defined at the First Vatican Council (1869-1870)?
questions.answer: Papal infallibility

questions.question: When was the Second Vatican Council held?
How many ecumenical councils does the Catholic Church recognize?
What was decided at the Council of Jerusalem described in Acts 15?
questions.answer: Gentile converts did not need to be circumcised or follow all Jewish laws

questions.question: What council established the Nicene Creed?
questions.answer: The First Council of Nicaea (in 325 AD)

questions.question: Who is traditionally considered the first Pope?
questions.answer: Saint Peter

questions.question: Who was the longest-reigning pope in history?
questions.answer: Pope Pius IX (31 years, 1846-1878)

questions.question: Which pope was reigning during the Protestant Reformation?
questions.answer: Pope Leo X

questions.question: Who was the first non-Italian pope in 455 years?
questions.answer: Pope John Paul II (from Poland)

questions.question: Which pope called the Second Vatican Council?
questions.answer: Pope John XXIII

questions.question: What is significant about Pope Francis being elected in 2013?
questions.answer: He was the first Jesuit pope and the first from the Americas

questions.question: Where is the official residence of the Pope?
questions.answer: Vatican City

questions.question: What body elects a new pope?
questions.answer: The College of Cardinals (in a conclave)

questions.question: What does white smoke from the Sistine Chapel chimney signify?
questions.answer: A new pope has been elected

questions.question: At which council was the doctrine of papal infallibility defined?
questions.answer: The First Vatican Council (1870)

questions.question: Who founded the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints?
questions.answer: Joseph Smith

questions.question: In what year was the Book of Mormon first published?
Which city is the headquarters of the LDS Church?
questions.answer: Salt Lake City, Utah

questions.question: Who led the Mormon pioneers to Utah?
questions.answer: Brigham Young

questions.question: Which angel appeared to Joseph Smith according to LDS teaching?
questions.answer: Angel Moroni

questions.question: What are LDS temple garments?
questions.answer: Sacred underclothing worn by endowed members of the LDS Church

questions.question: What is the Word of Wisdom in LDS faith?
questions.answer: A health code that prohibits alcohol, tobacco, coffee, and tea

questions.question: How long is a typical LDS missionary service?
questions.answer: 2 years for men, 18 months for women

questions.question: What is the Pearl of Great Price?
questions.answer: One of the four standard works (scriptures) of the LDS Church

questions.question: What is the Quorum of the Twelve Apostles in the LDS Church?
questions.answer: A governing body second only to the First Presidency in the church hierarchy

questions.question: What is a torii gate in Shintoism?
questions.answer: A traditional Japanese gate marking the entrance to a Shinto shrine

questions.question: What are kami in Shintoism?
questions.answer: Spirits or deities in the Shinto religion

questions.question: What country is Shintoism primarily associated with?
questions.answer: Japan

questions.question: What is Amaterasu in Shintoism?
questions.answer: The sun goddess and one of the most important deities in Shinto

questions.question: What is misogi in Shintoism?
questions.answer: A ritual purification practice, often involving water

questions.question: What are the Four Noble Truths?
questions.answer: The foundation of Buddhist teaching about suffering and the path to enlightenment

questions.question: What was the Buddha's name before his enlightenment?
questions.answer: Siddhartha Gautama

questions.question: What is nirvana in Buddhism?
questions.answer: The state of enlightenment and liberation from the cycle of rebirth

questions.question: What is the Eightfold Path in Buddhism?
questions.answer: The Buddhist path to end suffering, including right understanding, speech, action, and other practices

questions.question: Who is the Dalai Lama?
questions.answer: The spiritual leader of Tibetan Buddhism

questions.question: What are the Five Pillars of Islam?
questions.answer: The five fundamental practices: Shahada (faith), Salat (prayer), Zakat (charity), Sawm (fasting), and Hajj (pilgrimage)

questions.question: What is the holy book of Islam?
questions.answer: The Quran (or Koran)

questions.question: Who is the prophet and founder of Islam?
questions.answer: Muhammad (Prophet Muhammad)

questions.question: What is the holiest city in Islam?
questions.answer: Mecca (in Saudi Arabia)

questions.question: What is Ramadan?
questions.answer: The Islamic holy month of fasting from dawn to sunset

questions.question: Which Taoist text contains the saying 'The journey of a thousand miles begins with a single step'?
questions.answer: Tao Te Ching (or Dao De Jing)

questions.question: According to the Tao Te Ching, what is meant by 'He who knows does not speak. He who speaks does not know'?
questions.answer: True wisdom is found in silence and humility, not in talking

questions.question: What does the Tao Te Ching mean by 'When I let go of what I am, I become what I might be'?
questions.answer: Releasing attachment to the ego allows transformation and growth

questions.question: The Tao Te Ching states 'The wise man is one who knows what he does not know.' What philosophy does this reflect?
questions.answer: Humility and acknowledgment of the limits of knowledge

questions.question: What does 'Nature does not hurry, yet everything is accomplished' from the Tao Te Ching teach?
questions.answer: The value of patience and working in harmony with natural timing

questions.question: According to the Tao Te Ching, what is meant by 'The softest things in the world overcome the hardest things'?
questions.answer: Flexibility and gentleness can be more powerful than force and rigidity (like water wearing down stone)
